# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Release

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

  create_release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Create release on GitHub
        id: create_release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
          release_name: Release ${{ github.ref }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: false

  publish_npm:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: create_release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Publish to npm
        run: npm publish

  publish_pypi:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: create_release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install twine wheel

      - name: Publish to PyPI
        run: |
          python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel
          twine upload dist/*

  cleanup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: publish_npm, publish_pypi
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Clean up
        run: |
          rm -rf dist
          rm -rf build
